tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, high protein, glucose, and ketones) indicate significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, raising concern for renal dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c suggests chronic hypoglycemia or anemia. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or urinary tract pathology.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
